description: Site Performance Optimization - Reduce load times with compression, caching, N+1 fixes, batch dashboard endpoint, and React Query

userStories.title: Add response compression to API
userStories.description: As a user, I want API responses to be compressed so that data transfers are faster, especially on slower connections.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure `compression` middleware in Express API (services/api/src/index.ts)
Verify responses include `Content-Encoding: gzip` header when client sends `Accept-Encoding: gzip`
Existing API tests still pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cache-control headers to read endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want appropriate cache-control headers on API responses so that clients and intermediaries can cache responses and reduce redundant requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create reusable Express middleware `cacheControl(seconds)` in services/api/src/middleware/
Add `Cache-Control: private, max-age=30` to stable read endpoints (`/burn-buddies`, `/burn-squads`, `/friends`)
Add `Cache-Control: private, max-age=5` to frequently-changing endpoints (`/workouts/partner-active`, `/group-workouts`)
Add `Cache-Control: no-store` to sensitive endpoints (`/users/me`)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix N+1 queries in partner-active endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want the partner activity check to load fast so I can see who's working out without waiting.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor `GET /workouts/partner-active` to batch Firestore queries instead of querying per-buddy in a loop
Use Firestore `in` operator to query up to 30 UIDs at once, with chunking for larger sets
Same response shape and data as before (no breaking changes)
Existing tests pass; add test for batched query path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix N+1 queries in user profile endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want profile pages to load quickly instead of waiting for sequential database lookups.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor `GET /users/:uid/profile` to batch group workout queries using Firestore `in` operator instead of per-referenceId loop
Batch friend/buddy profile lookups into a single multi-get instead of individual doc reads
Same response shape and data as before (no breaking changes)
Existing tests pass; add test covering the batched query path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create batch dashboard endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a single API endpoint that returns all the data the dashboard needs so the home page can load with 1 request instead of 9+.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `GET /dashboard` endpoint in a new route file services/api/src/routes/dashboard.ts
Return combined payload: { user, burnBuddies, burnSquads, groupWorkouts, buddyRequests: { incoming, outgoing }, squadJoinRequests, activeWorkout, partnerActivity }
Use `Promise.all` to fetch all data in parallel within the endpoint (not sequential)
Enrich buddy/squad profiles server-side (include display names, streak data) to eliminate N+M client-side enrichment calls
Register the route in services/api/src/index.ts behind requireAuth middleware
Add tests using the existing vi.mock + vi.hoisted pattern with supertest
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Firestore `in` operator limit is 30 values. Chunk queries for users with more than 30 buddies/squad members.

userStories.title: Add React Query to the web app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a data-fetching/caching library so that API responses are cached, deduplicated, and background-refreshed automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Install `@tanstack/react-query` in apps/web
Create a QueryClientProvider client component wrapper with defaults: staleTime: 30_000, gcTime: 300_000, refetchOnWindowFocus: true
Add the provider wrapper to apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx
Create apps/web/src/lib/queries.ts with typed query hooks (useDashboard, useProfile) that use the existing API client from lib/api.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Layout is a server component, so QueryClientProvider must be a separate client component wrapper.

userStories.title: Migrate dashboard page to batch endpoint + React Query
userStories.description: As a user, I want the home page to load in under 1 second (cached) so the app feels instant.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the individual useEffect fetch calls in the dashboard page.tsx with a single useDashboard() query hook
Show a loading skeleton/spinner while data loads (no blank screen)
Navigating away and back shows cached data immediately (stale-while-revalidate)
Remove the old loadData function and individual state variables that are replaced
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Optimize polling strategy with React Query
userStories.description: As a user, I want real-time partner activity updates without the app burning unnecessary resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace any manual setInterval polling with React Query's built-in refetchInterval: 30_000 on the partner-active query
Only poll when the browser tab is focused (refetchIntervalInBackground: false)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create web vitals API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an API endpoint to receive Core Web Vitals metrics so I can track real-world performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `POST /metrics/vitals` endpoint in a new route file services/api/src/routes/metrics.ts
Endpoint accepts JSON body with metric name (LCP, FCP, TTFB, CLS, INP), value, and optional metadata
Log received metrics via pino as structured JSON
Register the route in services/api/src/index.ts (no auth required — metrics are anonymous)
Add basic tests using the existing vi.mock pattern
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add web-vitals reporting to web app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to measure Core Web Vitals in the browser so I can track the real-world impact of performance improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: Install the `web-vitals` library in apps/web
Create apps/web/src/lib/vitals.ts that captures LCP, FCP, TTFB, CLS, and INP metrics
In production: send metrics to the API via `POST /metrics/vitals` (fire-and-forget, non-blocking)
In development: log metrics to the browser console in a readable format
Wire up the vitals reporter in a client component loaded from app/layout.tsx
Typecheck passes
